{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun burial mound in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[burial mound]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Tumulus is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun burial mound in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[burial mound]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Tumulus is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun burial mound in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[burial mound]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Tumulus is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Tumulus is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. {{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} Sendō Kofun [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sendō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sendō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Chikuzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Sendō Kofun]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sendō Kofun]] [[en:Sendō Kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} Sendō Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} Sendō Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sendō Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period decorated kofun Kofun in Chikuzen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sendō Kofun | native_name =仙道古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sendo Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Sendō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sendō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|27|N|130|38|25|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[:en:Jade|Jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=90 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.chikuzen.fukuoka.jp/S044/010/030/20180212182255.html Chikuzen Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12795 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} Sendō Kofun

The {{nihongo|'''Sendō Kofun'''|仙道古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Hisamitsu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/191982|title=仙道古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located in the northern part of the Chikugo Plain, at the foot of the Amidagamine Hills, which extends south from the Asakura Mountains. Approximately 50 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli, in the area make up the Amidagamine Kofun Cluster, and Sendō Kofun is the largest of these. [[Archaeological excavation]] in 1977 revealed that it was a two-tiered tumulus with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the surface, and that it has a diameter of about 33 meters and height of four meters. It is surrounded by a double moat, two to three meters wide and about one meter deep. The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is orientated to the southwest, and was robbed in antiquity. The burial chamber is located in the center of the mound, and is 2 to 2.4 meters wide and 3 meters long, and the floor is covered with paving stones. Decorative murals cover the entire surface of the burial chamber. The patterns are geometric, such as circles and triangles, and the colors red and green are used. The only remaining evidence of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was some small fragments of [[:en:Jade|Jade]], possibly from a bracelet. However, anthropomorphic ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' of warriors carrying shields and horses were discovered from the outer periphery of the mound, indicating that the tomb was constructed around the 6th century. It is a unique tumulus in that decorative ''kofun'' do not normally have ''haniwa''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] and the burial chamber is open to the public every spring and fall.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is located 5.3 kilometers (about 15-minutes by car) northwest of [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].
